issues.description: Email validation regex is overly permissive and may allow invalid email addresses (e.g., multiple dots, special characters in local part)
issues.suggestion: Use a more robust email validation regex or a dedicated email validation library like validator.js

issues.description: HTML entity encoding is incomplete - missing encoding for other potentially dangerous characters like backticks, forward slashes, and does not handle all XSS vectors
issues.suggestion: Use a dedicated library like DOMPurify or the OWASP ESAPI for comprehensive XSS prevention, or encode all special characters systematically

issues.description: ID generation using Date.now() and Math.random() is not cryptographically secure and may produce collisions in high-concurrency scenarios
issues.suggestion: Use crypto.randomUUID() or a library like uuid (v4) for guaranteed unique IDs

issues.description: Email is not sanitized before storage, only lowercased. Email could contain special characters that might cause issues in certain contexts
issues.suggestion: Consider sanitizing the email input as well, or at minimum validate that it doesn't contain control characters

issues.description: Input validation uses loose falsy checks which would reject empty strings but also 0, false, etc. (though not applicable for strings)
issues.suggestion: Use more explicit validation: if (!input.email?.trim() || !input.name?.trim()) for better clarity and to handle whitespace-only inputs

issues.description: Database query is commented out, making the function not fully functional
issues.suggestion: Either implement the actual database call or add a clear TODO comment indicating this is a stub for testing purposes

issues.description: No error handling for the database operation in saveUser - errors would propagate uncaught
issues.suggestion: Add try-catch block with appropriate error handling or logging for database operations

issues.description: Missing input length validation - extremely long names or emails could cause issues
issues.suggestion: Add maximum length validation for email and name fields (e.g., email max 254 chars, name max 100 chars)

codeQuality.summary: Well-structured code with good security practices (parameterized queries, input sanitization), but has medium-severity issues with ID generation collision risk and regex-based email validation that could be more robust.

untestedPaths.reasoning: Email validation is a security-critical function that determines whether user input is accepted; incorrect validation can lead to invalid data in the system or rejection of legitimate emails
untestedPaths.suggestedTest: Test valid emails (user@example.com, user.name+tag@example.co.uk), invalid emails (missing @, missing domain, spaces, special characters), and edge cases (empty string, very long emails, international characters)

untestedPaths.reasoning: XSS prevention is a critical security function; failure to properly sanitize input could allow script injection attacks
untestedPaths.suggestedTest: Test XSS attack vectors: , , strings with quotes and double quotes, mixed HTML entities, and verify output escapes <>&"' correctly

untestedPaths.reasoning: Primary public API function that handles user creation with validation and database persistence; needs comprehensive testing of all validation paths and error scenarios
untestedPaths.suggestedTest: Test successful user creation with valid input, missing email error, missing name error, invalid email format error, verify email is lowercased, verify name is sanitized, and verify async database save is called with correct parameters

untestedPaths.reasoning: Error handling branch that prevents invalid data entry; must verify proper error message and exception throwing
untestedPaths.suggestedTest: Test createUser with input {email: '', name: 'John'} and verify it throws 'Email and name are required' error

untestedPaths.reasoning: Error handling branch that prevents invalid data entry; must verify proper error message and exception throwing
untestedPaths.suggestedTest: Test createUser with input {email: 'user@test.com', name: ''} and verify it throws 'Email and name are required' error

untestedPaths.reasoning: Validates email format before user creation; must verify rejection of malformed emails
untestedPaths.suggestedTest: Test createUser with input {email: 'invalid-email', name: 'John'} and verify it throws 'Invalid email format' error

untestedPaths.reasoning: Email normalization to lowercase prevents duplicate users with different casing; needs verification
untestedPaths.suggestedTest: Test createUser with email 'User@Example.COM' and verify the returned user object has email 'user@example.com'

untestedPaths.reasoning: ID generation must produce unique identifiers; collisions could cause data corruption or overwrites
untestedPaths.suggestedTest: Test generateId produces IDs with 'user_' prefix, contains timestamp, contains random component, and verify multiple calls produce different IDs

untestedPaths.reasoning: Database persistence function using parameterized queries for SQL injection prevention; needs verification of correct query structure and parameter binding
untestedPaths.suggestedTest: Test saveUser is called with correct parameterized query format and parameters array in correct order [id, email, name, createdAt], verify no string concatenation in query

untestedPaths.reasoning: XSS sanitization with multiple attack vectors in single string needs verification
untestedPaths.suggestedTest: Test sanitizeInput with '<script>alert("test")</script>' and verify all characters are properly escaped to '<script>alert("test")</script>'

untestedPaths.reasoning: Email regex may behave unexpectedly with edge cases that should be explicitly tested
untestedPaths.suggestedTest: Test isValidEmail with edge cases: consecutive dots (user..name@test.com), starting/ending with dot (.user@test.com), email with + symbol (user+tag@test.com), subdomain (user@mail.example.com)

untestedPaths.reasoning: Async function may have race conditions with ID generation or database saves
untestedPaths.suggestedTest: Test multiple concurrent createUser calls with Promise.all and verify all users get unique IDs and all database saves complete successfully

untestedPaths.reasoning: Double-escaping could occur if input is already escaped; behavior should be documented
untestedPaths.suggestedTest: Test sanitizeInput with already escaped input '<script>' and verify it becomes '&lt;script&gt;' (double escaped) to document this behavior

untestedPaths.reasoning: createdAt timestamp should reflect user creation time accurately
untestedPaths.suggestedTest: Test createUser returns a user with createdAt within 1 second of current time to verify timestamp accuracy

testCoverage.summary: No test coverage exists for fixtures/clean-code.ts; all 5 functions (3 exported, 2 internal) including critical security functions (email validation, XSS sanitization) and the main user creation API are completely untested.

suggestions.description: Extract validation logic into a dedicated function to improve testability and separation of concerns
suggestions.benefits: Improves testability, separation of concerns, and makes validation logic reusable. Easier to extend with additional validation rules.

suggestions.description: Replace Date.now() and Math.random() with crypto.randomUUID() for better uniqueness guarantees
suggestions.benefits: Provides cryptographically secure random IDs with better collision resistance. More concise and modern approach.

suggestions.description: Use a Map-based approach for better maintainability and extensibility of character replacements
suggestions.benefits: Reduces redundant replace calls, makes it easier to add/modify character replacements, and improves performance with a single regex pass.

suggestions.description: Inline the regex pattern directly in the return statement for simpler code
suggestions.benefits: Simplifies the function by removing an unnecessary intermediate variable, making it more concise without sacrificing readability.

suggestions.description: Add error handling wrapper to return Result type pattern instead of throwing exceptions
suggestions.before:
export async function createUser(input: CreateUserInput): Promise<User> {
  // throws Error
suggestions.benefits: Makes error handling explicit and type-safe, avoiding exceptions. Callers are forced to handle both success and failure cases at compile time.

suggestions.description: Extract user object construction into a factory function for better testability
suggestions.benefits: Improves testability by isolating object construction logic. Makes it easier to mock or modify user creation behavior.

refactorings.summary: Found 6 refactoring opportunities including extracting validation logic, modernizing ID generation with crypto.randomUUID(), improving sanitization with a map-based approach, simplifying email validation, adopting Result type pattern for error handling, and extracting user construction into a factory function.

recommendations.description: Add comprehensive test coverage for security-critical functions including email validation (isValidEmail) and XSS prevention (sanitizeInput). These functions have 0% test coverage despite being essential for application security.

Replace the custom ID generation function with crypto.randomUUID() to prevent potential ID collisions in high-concurrency scenarios. The current implementation using Date.now() and Math.random() is not cryptographically secure.
Implement tests for all error handling branches in createUser function, including validation failures for missing email/name and invalid email format. These paths are currently untested.
Consider using a more robust email validation library (like validator.js) instead of a simple regex pattern. The current regex is overly permissive and may allow invalid email formats.
Extract validation logic into a separate validateUserInput() function to improve testability and separation of concerns. This will make the codebase more maintainable.
Use a dedicated XSS prevention library like DOMPurify for more comprehensive input sanitization. The current implementation only handles basic HTML entities.
